comment.body:
Reversion levels sign potential reversion points with different probability. 
How? -They are derived from the volatility. That's what the plp are pricing in.
So if the market goes out of that range and stabilizes outside, thats a clear sign that market is diverged from market expectations... it is kinda "out-of-control" in terms of traders sentiment. also causing losses for MMs... not necessary but most likely. 
The key is, that you can read the sentiment if the momentum is aligned with the customers expectations or it is something out of them

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
Vol derived lvls, and also zero vanna, gives you a picture about the vol profile... whats gonna be realized and whats not... it reveals the customer flow.
I explained already. IV = supply/demand, realized vol = not just a bet anymore but pure reality.
You derive the vol that should happen if the move is aligning with the positioning. 
But ongoing and realized stubborn divergences reveal the actual flows.
comment.raw_body:

comment.body:
and there are other lvls that are important from momentum perspective... especially in high vol times... thats where old-school approaches come into play like gaps, and VWAP etc.

This is why I marked the lvl when Trump announced the 90 days pause as cap.

So the key is that different lvls provide you different informations for the big puzzle. 
Any lvl can be broken or  rejected... different probabilities..
But all of them provide you infos
